meaning: n.破裂,骨折v.(使)破碎,(使)破裂
examples: I had a stress fracture in my left shin.
Old people's bones are more prone to fracture.
He suffered a hairline fracture of the right index finger.
Its "fracture-critical" design means that if any part fails the whole bridge could fail, experts said.
She says it's implausible that the drugs would have no fracture effect on women with osteopenia.
